records.title: Dynamic task and resource scheduling for space-air-ground-sea integrated networks

structured_summary.core_concept: Jointly schedule task offloading, satellite resources, UAV bandwidth and terrestrial compute while anticipating handovers and congestion.
structured_summary.gs_linkops_relevance: Extends pass scheduling into an end-to-end task-routing model where downlink capacity, compute location, handover state and delivery deadline are co-optimized.
structured_summary.reported_result: The paper reports at least 23% lower average task delay than selected benchmark methods under its simulation conditions; this is not an operational SLA.

pending_confirmation: Performance under real ground-station capacity conflicts

sources.title: Dynamic Task and Resource Scheduling Towards Green Space-Air-Ground-Sea Integrated Network

records.title: Concurrent optical ground infrastructure for satellite-serviced quantum networks

structured_summary.core_concept: Quantum satellite services require simultaneous multi-hop path availability because entanglement cannot be copied and practical buffering is limited.
structured_summary.gs_linkops_relevance: Introduces a scheduling class where a contact is valid only when multiple optical ground stations and satellite links are concurrently available, rather than independently bookable passes.
structured_summary.architecture_findings: The study identifies ground-station lattice geometry, multi-inclination constellations, multi-party satellite service policies and satellite altitude as major architecture variables.

sources.title: Designing a Satellite Serviced Quantum Network Backbone for Concurrent Global Connectivity

records.title: Authenticated encryption for emergency space telemetry

structured_summary.core_concept: Apply lightweight authenticated encryption with fixed-length messages to protect confidentiality, integrity and authenticity while preserving compatibility with constrained telemetry transports.
structured_summary.gs_linkops_relevance: Adds security evidence and message-protection capabilities to the ground-station interface model, especially for emergency telemetry and command-stack protection workflows.
structured_summary.standards_context: The study frames its design against command stack protection requirements in NASA-STD-1006A.
